content.full_text:
T= 84-041

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SACRAMENTO                                    THE HARTFORD BUILDING
1825 EIGHTH STREET, SUITE 550                650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814                 SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752                     TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800
                                                                        FILE NUMBER



                            February 27, 1984


Janis Shank McLean, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

          Re: Valuation of Fundraising Tickets Provided
              to Public Officials

Dear Ms. McLean:

     Pursuant to our phone conversation, I wish to
restate the advice you gave me.

     I described a situation in which Corporation A
has purchased a table of tickets to a fundraising dinner
for Candidate X. Public Official B has been invited as
the guest of Corporation A to that fundraising event. The
question is whether or not Corporation A has made a gift
to Public Official B for the value of the fundraising dinner
ticket.

     You advised me that it has been the opinion of the
staff of the FPPC that the value of the ticket to a fundraiser
is nothing. Therefore, if Corporation A invites Public
Official B to sit at its table at a fundraising dinner for
Candidate X, no gift, as defined in Government Code Section
82028, has been made by Corporation A or accepted by Public
Official B and therefore Public Official B has no reporting
responsibilities under Government Code Section 87207(a)(1)
by reason of his attendance at the fundraising dinner.

     Please confirm our conversation by signing the
attached letter copy and returning it to me. If I have misstated
any part of our conversation, please advise me at your earliest
convenience.

                                    Kind regards,


                                    Kirk Alan Lesener
                                    Administrator
                                    Political Reports Unit


Janis Shank McLean
Janis Shank McLean, Esq.

sections.question_synthetic: Does a corporation providing a fundraising dinner ticket to a public official as a guest constitute a reportable gift under Government Code Section 82028?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence seeking FPPC confirmation of prior verbal advice rather than an official FPPC advice letter response. The letter references advice already given by FPPC staff but does not contain a formal FPPC conclusion or response. No formal Q/A sections present; question and advice are stated informally within the letter body.

Legal Division Fair Political Practices Commission Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 Re: Valuation of Fundraising Tickets Provided to Public Officials Dear Ms. McLean: Pursuant to our phone conversation, I wish to restate the advice you gave me. I described a situation in which Corporation A has purchased a table of tickets to a fundraising dinner for Candidate X. Public Official B has been invited as the guest of Corporation A to that fundraising event. The question is whether or not Corporation A has made a gift to Public Official B for the value of the fundraising dinner ticket. You advised me that it has been the opinion of the staff of the FPPC that the value of the ticket to a fundraiser is nothing. Therefore, if Corporation A invites Public Official B to sit at its table at a fundraising dinner for Candidate X, no gift, as defined in Government Code Section 82028, has been made by Corporation A or accepted by Public Official B and therefore Public Official B has no reporting responsibilities under Government Code Section 87207(a)(1) by reason of his attendance at the fundraising dinner. Please confirm our conversation by signing the attached letter copy and returning it to me. If I have misstated any part of our conversation, please advise me at your earliest convenience. Kind regards, Kirk Alan Lesener Administrator Political Reports Unit Janis Shank McLean Janis Shank McLean, Esq.
embedding.summary: An attorney writes to the FPPC's Legal Division requesting confirmation of prior phone advice that the value of fundraising dinner tickets provided by a corporation to a public official as a guest is nothing and therefore does not constitute a reportable gift.
